raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing of the proposed GenoReason framework.","R1.2":"Strengthening the artifact requires checking that the argument from bottleneck to method to results to contribution is coherent.","R1.3":"The request implicates whether reported performance gains and dataset details adequately support the claims made in the draft.","R1.6":"The most direct responsibility is improving sentence- and paragraph-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.","RX.1":"Any projection of strengthening should preserve uncertainty around unsupported methodological or empirical details.","RX.2":"The draft contains potentially strong novelty and clinical-impact claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.","RX.3":"The responsibility must remain limited to improving the paper text rather than executing experiments or validating external facts.","RX.4":"Because the topic concerns clinical genetics, strengthening must avoid wording that could imply unsafe clinical reliability.","RX.5":"Claims about evidence sources, evaluation, code release, and reproducibility require traceable grounding in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Making the draft stronger centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing of the proposed GenoReason framework.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the artifact requires checking that the argument from bottleneck to method to results to contribution is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: The request implicates whether reported performance gains and dataset details adequately support the claims made in the draft.
rationale.R1.6: The most direct responsibility is improving sentence- and paragraph-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of strengthening should preserve uncertainty around unsupported methodological or empirical details.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains potentially strong novelty and clinical-impact claims, so avoiding overclaiming is active.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility must remain limited to improving the paper text rather than executing experiments or validating external facts.
rationale.RX.4: Because the topic concerns clinical genetics, strengthening must avoid wording that could imply unsafe clinical reliability.
rationale.RX.5: Claims about evidence sources, evaluation, code release, and reproducibility require traceable grounding in the artifact.
